subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, Eurostat, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, BTI) + Croatian official (DZS, Croatian National Bank) + Croatian/independent press.
A EUROZONE (since 2023) and Schengen member; a stable EU democracy with a sharply improved fiscal trajectory.

Standout features: a falling debt ratio, sound banks, NATO/EU anchoring, and low crime, against severe demographic decline, climate/seismic exposure, and entrenched corruption. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone/multilateral.

economic.notes: A sharply improved fiscal trajectory, sound banks, and euro membership, with tourism concentration a mild risk.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~57% GDP, down sharply from a ~87% peak, with a deficit ~2.9% (procyclical spending a flag). (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - credible but not sovereign. (ECB/HNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Uses the euro, the world's second reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: HICP ~4.4-4.6%, above the ECB target, with easing expected. (Eurostat; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Low NPLs and high capitalisation, with stress tests showing above-EU-average resilience. (HNB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Exports ~half of GDP, tourism-heavy plus manufacturing and niche shipbuilding, EU-integrated, no resource curse; tourism concentration a mild risk. (IMF; WTTC)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics and EU-fund absorption, against persistent corruption and a slow judiciary.

rule_of_law.notes: An EU member and regional leader on the WJP index, but with persistent corruption and a slow judiciary. (TI; WJP; EC Rule of Law 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: DZS is credible and Eurostat-compliant. (DZS; Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Functional and absorbing EU recovery funds. (EC)

press_freedom.notes: Free, but with SLAPPs and ownership/political pressure. (RSF; EC Rule of Law 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable EU democracy with strong liberties, against severe demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A stable democracy on a third HDZ-led term; corruption scandals but orderly. (BTI 2026)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: EU/Schengen with lawful routes and labour-importing for tourism and construction. (OECD 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Relatively cohesive, with minor war-legacy/Serb-minority and regional gaps. (BTI)

demographics.notes: Lost ~20% of the population since independence, with mass post-accession emigration of the young and skilled and heavy ageing - a severe long-term drag. (DZS; OSW)

geopolitical.notes: A reliably Western-aligned NATO/EU member with no active conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO and EU member, reliably Western-aligned. (NATO; EC)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict in a stable region. (Global Peace Index)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Good tourism-grade infrastructure and low crime, against climate and seismic exposure.

climate.notes: Warming above the global average with wildfire, drought, and flood, plus seismic risk (the 2020 Zagreb/Petrinja quakes). (OECD 2026; World Bank)

healthcare.notes: Universal and decent, with a nurse shortage and a doctor-exodus strain. (WHO Europe)

infrastructure.notes: Good and EU-funded, with strong tourism infrastructure. (EC; WTTC)

crime_safety.notes: Low, with homicide under 1/100k - a strength. (GPI; State Dept)

disaster_insurance.notes: An active seismic/flood market but with moderate penetration versus exposure. (World Bank/GFDRR)

summary: A stable EU democracy and eurozone/Schengen member with a sharply improved fiscal trajectory (debt down from a ~87% peak to ~57%), sound, well-capitalised banks, an export- and tourism-driven economy, reliable NATO/EU anchoring, and low crime. The drags are a severe demographic decline (a ~20% population loss since independence on mass post-accession emigration), entrenched corruption and a slow judiciary, and real climate and seismic exposure. Euro membership pools monetary policy at a credible ECB.
